title: Sulla flexuosa
taxonomy: Sulla flexuosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Sulla flexuosa is further broken down into its genus Sulla, which is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. Sulla flexuosa is closely related to other species in the genus Sulla, and its classification is well established in botanical research. intro: Sulla flexuosa, also known as Sulla or Italian clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe, Asia, and Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sulla flexuosa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
